title: content.rs replacement overlap filter drops second match when ranges are adjacent
description: In content.rs process_content(), the overlap-filter loop keeps a replacement only if r.1 <= prev.0 (current end <= previous start). This is checked on the sorted-descending list after reversing, so 'prev' is actually the last kept replacement with a higher start position. The condition should be r.1 <= prev.0, which it is, but this means two adjacent replacements where one ends exactly where the other begins are incorrectly treated as non-overlapping and both kept, potentially producing malformed HTML when the second replacement's replacement text contains the boundary character that was also the start of the first. Specifically: a URL immediately followed by a hashtag (no space) produces a double-replacement of the shared boundary byte.
close_reason: False positive: adjacent ranges (start == prev_end) are correctly kept by strict less-than.
